 

Standard Operating Procedure  

For Milli-Q Advantage A10 Water Purification System, by Millipore 

 

 

In the University of Maryland Greenebaum Comprehensive Cancer Center Flow Cytometry Shared Services, UMGCCC FCSS 

 

Bressler Research Building, BRB 7-022 

 

 

 

 

​​Underwood, Karen​ 

​​1-28-2020​ 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

UMGCCC FCSS 

Dr. Xiaoxuan Fan 

 

Table of Contents 

 

 

Section										Page Number 

1.0	Objective										2 

2.0	Scope											2 

3.0	Responsibilities									3 

4.0	Definitions										3 

5.0	Purpose										4 

6.0	Operating Procedure								4 

7.0	Maintenance										6 

8.0	Safety											6 

9.0	References										7 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

UMGCCC FCSS 

Dr. Xiaoxuan Fan 

 

Standard Operation Procedure for Milli-Q Advantage A10 Water Purification System Operation 

 

1.0	Objective: 

This SOP outlines the objective for the use of Milli-Q Water Purification System to produce purified water at the University of Maryland Comprehensive Cancer Center Flow Cytometry Shared Services, UMGCCC FCSS.  The Advantage A10, serial #F3AA35817A, located in BRB 7-022.   

2.0	Scope: 

This establishes an SOP for applicable operation of the Milli-Q water system from       Millipore.  This is for all lab staff, users, interns, any person who may use the facility.   

3.0	Responsibility: 

 

3.1       Principal Investigator:  The Principal Investigator, PI, is responsible for ensuring  

personnel under their supervision are trained on the safe and proper use of the  

Milli-Q Water Purification System and that correct procedures are followed.  

 

3.2	Designated person:  The PI may designate a member of the lab to oversee the correct operation and maintenance of the Milli-Q Water Purification System.  This person shall: 

 

Direct all users in proper usage of the Milli-Q Water Purification System and keep it in good working condition. All users are to report any defects, breakdowns or quality control issues to the facility PI and/or the core staff. 

 

 

4.0	Definitions: 

 

4.1	Milli-Q Water A10 Purification System:  was designed to deliver high quality ultrapure water. 

 

 

 

 

 

UMGCCC FCSS 

Dr. Xiaoxuan Fan 

 

 

5.0	Purpose: 

 

The purpose of the Milli-Q A10 Water Purification System, by Millipore, is to deliver high quality ultra-pure water.  The system features two separate and distinct components:  the production unit and the delivery point-of-use unit. 

 

 

 Operating Procedure: 

 

6.1	Diagrams: 

 

 

 

 

 

UMGCCC FCSS 

Dr. Xiaoxuan Fan 

 

 

 

6.2	How to get water from the Milli-Q, Production Unit 

a.	Use the Right Keypad button to move to the next screen. Use the  Left Keypad button to move to the previous screen. Use the  Up Keypad to scroll up the menu. Use the  Down Keypad to scroll down the menu. Use the  Validate Keypad button to confirm a parameter modification or selection. 

 

To optimized water quality, product water can be recirculated with the system before dispensing by selecting “Recirculation” and press . Wait till product water quality is optimized and press . 

 

To dispense water, press the POD (Point of Delivery) Unit Plunger down. The Milli-Q will start dispensing water. The parameter 18.2 MΩ.cm @25oC. 

 

UMGCCC FCSS 

Dr. Xiaoxuan Fan 

 

 

When you have finished dispensing water, press the POD Unit Plunger again.  

 

Leave the Milli-Q in READY mode. Do not leave it in STANDBY mode unless you are going to perform maintenance on the system. 

 

6.3	How to dispense an exact amount of product water, delivery point-of-use. 

a.	Ensure the system is in READY mode. 

b.	Select “Volume” and press . Select the desired volume of water   to be delivered and press . When the volumetric dispensing is done, the system recirculates water for 3 minutes. 

7.0. 	Maintenance: 

7.1	Performed by trained lab personnel in collaboration with Millipore Service Engineer, Milli-Q Support:  888-645-5478, labwaterservicesrequests@merck.group.com 

7.2	Pod Pack is changed every 3 months using TISCH PES Capsule Filter:   0.22 um, 67 mm, 90 m long.  The capsule filter #6790M/Part#SF14920.   

7.3	Quantum Cartridge:   replaced every 12 months 

7.4	A-10 Lamp:  replaced every 12 months 

7.5	Q Guard T2 Pack replaced every 12 months 

7.6 	185 NM Lamp replaced every 24 months. 

7.7	Every 12 months after replacing A-10 lamp, Q Guard T2 Pack, and Quantum Guard.  This is a closed system so to clean drain water in the reservoir tank by the tap by the reservoir. Open the knob of the reservoir at the bottom, which is blue in color, to drain off remaining water. 

8.0	Safety Precautions 

8.1	Wear lab coat and gloves to handle the equipment. 

 

UMGCCC FCSS 

Dr. Xiaoxuan Fan 

 

8.2	Avoid splashing of water and overfilling containers as the floor may become flooded with water and become slippery. The electrical circuit may be tripped if water gets into any electrical appliances nearby. 

 

8.3	Use a cart to transport the filled carboy as it may be very heavy. 

 

9.0. 	References:  

 

7.1 	Milli-Q Reference System 

https://physiology.case.edu/media/eq_manuals/eq_manual_milli-q_reference_user_manual.pdf 

 

7.2	Millipore website 

http://www.millipore.com/bioscience 

 

Written by:		Karen F. Underwood, MS, Research Lead Specialist 

Adapted by:		 

Effective date:	 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

UMGCCC FCSS 

Dr. Xiaoxuan Fan 

 

 

Approved by:		______________________________		___________________ 

Xiaoxuan Fanl, Ph.D., Director			Date 

 

Reviewed by:	_______________________________		____________________ 

Date 

_______________________________		____________________ 

Date 

_______________________________		____________________ 

Date 

 

 

MODIFICATIONS AFTER ORIGINAL IMPLEMENTATION 

 

Date: 

Description of Modification: 

Approved by:
